frames.narrationSummary: Initialize prevTwo at 0 so the take option has a clean base value.
Initialize prevOne at 0 so the best-so-far total also starts empty.
Check whether house 0 is the next transition to evaluate.
Move the focus to house 0 with value 2.
Compute take = prevTwo + nums[index] = 2.
Compute skip = prevOne = 0.
Compare take 2 against skip 0; choose take.
Shift prevTwo forward to the old prevOne value 0.
Commit prevOne = best = 2.
Check whether house 1 is the next transition to evaluate.
Move the focus to house 1 with value 7.
Compute take = prevTwo + nums[index] = 7.
Compute skip = prevOne = 2.
Compare take 7 against skip 2; choose take.
Shift prevTwo forward to the old prevOne value 2.
Commit prevOne = best = 7.
Check whether house 2 is the next transition to evaluate.
Move the focus to house 2 with value 9.
Compute take = prevTwo + nums[index] = 11.
Compute skip = prevOne = 7.
Compare take 11 against skip 7; choose take.
Shift prevTwo forward to the old prevOne value 7.
Commit prevOne = best = 11.
Check whether house 3 is the next transition to evaluate.
Move the focus to house 3 with value 3.
Compute take = prevTwo + nums[index] = 10.
Compute skip = prevOne = 11.
Compare take 10 against skip 11; choose skip.
Shift prevTwo forward to the old prevOne value 11.
Commit prevOne = best = 11.
Check whether house 4 is the next transition to evaluate.
Move the focus to house 4 with value 1.
Compute take = prevTwo + nums[index] = 12.
Compute skip = prevOne = 11.
Compare take 12 against skip 11; choose take.
Shift prevTwo forward to the old prevOne value 11.
Commit prevOne = best = 12.
Every house has been processed, so the rolling state now holds the answer.
Return the best non-adjacent total 12.
